answers:
**1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected/removed?**

No — several claims do not hold up:

- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default"** — false. `app/security/config.py:3-4` reads: `# Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic.` / `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`. The code explicitly defaults 2FA to off and opt-in, the opposite of what the doc claims. This must be corrected or removed — sending this to customers would misrepresent their account's security posture.
- **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF"** — not implemented. `app/routers/visits.py` only has a `GET /visits` endpoint returning an empty list, with a trailing `# TODO: pdf export` comment. There is no export route, no PDF generation code anywhere in the repo. This feature does not exist yet and must be removed from the draft.
- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows"** — unsubstantiated. A repo-wide search for "no-show"/"no_show" turns up only this sentence in the doc itself; there's no analytics, metric, or data source backing this number anywhere in the repository or git history (single commit, authored by "Fixture Bot", introduces the doc with this figure already baked in, no citation). This claim should be removed unless the author can point to an external data source not present in this repo.
- **"shown with production data"** (scheduling section) — contradicted by `captures.json`, which records both `before_scheduling.png` and `after_scheduling.png` as captured with `"state": "seed-demo"`. The screenshots are demo/seed data, not production data. Beyond being simply inaccurate, claiming "production data" for what is actually placeholder demo content is the kind of statement that could raise data-handling/privacy questions if taken literally — it should be corrected to say demo/seed data or removed.
- SMS reminders is the one claim that checks out: `app/notifications/sms.py` implements a `SmsSender.send_reminder` alongside the existing `app/notifications/email.py`, consistent with "reminders now by SMS too."

**2. The before/after scheduling calendar comparison — is it valid to send?**

No, it's broken and should not be sent as-is. Per `captures.json`, `after_scheduling.png` was captured from route `/billing/invoices`, not `/schedule` (the "before" shot's route). This is confirmed visually: `before_scheduling.png` literally renders the text "SCHEDULING CALENDAR" while `after_scheduling.png` renders "BILLING / INVOICES" — an entirely different page. The "after" image in the calendar comparison is not a calendar at all; it's a billing/invoices screen. Sending this would show customers a mismatched, nonsensical before/after pair.

**3. Reconciling the document's image references against files that actually exist:**

- `before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png` — all four files referenced exist on disk, so no broken links there. However `after_scheduling.png`'s *content* is wrong (see #2 — it's a billing/invoices capture mislabeled as the "after" scheduling shot).
- `after_export.png` — referenced in the "Export your visit history" section, but this file does **not** exist in `docs/release/img/` (only 5 PNGs exist, and none is named `after_export.png`). `captures.json` confirms this explicitly: `"after_export.png": {"route": "/visits/export", "state": "expected capture; no corresponding file"}`. This image link is broken and would render as a missing image in the email — and matches the fact that the export feature itself isn't implemented (see #1).
- `unused_capture_07.png` (renders "PATIENT LIST") exists in the img folder but is not referenced anywhere in the draft — it's an orphaned/leftover capture, not a document error per se, but worth cleaning up before shipping the folder.
- Also worth noting: the "before/after reminders" images are visually near-identical (both say "REMINDER SETTINGS"; after just adds a plain gray bar) — they don't visually communicate the new SMS capability, so the comparison is weak evidence even though the underlying feature claim is accurate.

**4. Anything else that shouldn't appear in a customer-facing message?**

Yes — the trailing line `Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance.` at the bottom of the doc (`highlights-v4.md:33`) is an internal drafting note, not customer-facing copy. It reads as an unpolished disclaimer/watermark that should never go out to clinic customers. It should be stripped before sending, along with the fact that this is explicitly labeled a "draft" — indicating it hasn't been through final review.
report:
## Plan
Verify the draft `docs/release/highlights-v4.md` (found in `clinic-portal/`) against the actual codebase: check whether each claimed feature (SMS reminders, redesigned calendar, PDF export, 2FA-by-default, 40%-fewer-no-shows stat) is implemented, and check whether the before/after screenshots actually depict what the doc says they do, using `captures.json` (the capture manifest) plus the images themselves.

## Actions
- `find` over the tree for release/v4 docs → found `clinic-portal/docs/release/highlights-v4.md`, `captures.json`, and 5 PNGs in `docs/release/img/`.
- `Read` `highlights-v4.md` — full draft text (33 lines).
- `Read` `captures.json` — manifest mapping each screenshot filename to the route/state it was captured from.
- `ls -la docs/release/img/` — confirmed exactly 5 PNG files exist: `after_reminders.png`, `after_scheduling.png`, `before_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png`, `unused_capture_07.png`. No `after_export.png`.
- `Read` (image) each of the 5 PNGs — each is a plain black-on-white text-label graphic (not an actual UI screenshot), rendering the strings: `before_scheduling.png` → "SCHEDULING CALENDAR", `after_scheduling.png` → "BILLING / INVOICES", `before_reminders.png` / `after_reminders.png` → "REMINDER SETTINGS" (after has a gray bar underneath), `unused_capture_07.png` → "PATIENT LIST".
- `find app tests -type f` — listed all source files: `app/notifications/email.py`, `app/notifications/sms.py`, `app/routers/visits.py`, `app/security/config.py`, `tests/test_notifications.py`.
- `Read` all four app files and the test file.
- `git log --oneline --all` and `git log -p` on the doc — single commit `24d8f6b` by "Fixture Bot", the doc was added whole; no history showing the "40% fewer no-shows" figure was ever computed or sourced.
- `Grep` repo-wide for "no-show"/"no_show" — only match is the doc itself; no supporting data, metric, or analytics code anywhere in the repo.

## Answers

**1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected/removed?**

No — several claims do not hold up:

- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default"** — false. `app/security/config.py:3-4` reads: `# Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic.` / `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`. The code explicitly defaults 2FA to off and opt-in, the opposite of what the doc claims. This must be corrected or removed — sending this to customers would misrepresent their account's security posture.
- **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF"** — not implemented. `app/routers/visits.py` only has a `GET /visits` endpoint returning an empty list, with a trailing `# TODO: pdf export` comment. There is no export route, no PDF generation code anywhere in the repo. This feature does not exist yet and must be removed from the draft.
- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows"** — unsubstantiated. A repo-wide search for "no-show"/"no_show" turns up only this sentence in the doc itself; there's no analytics, metric, or data source backing this number anywhere in the repository or git history (single commit, authored by "Fixture Bot", introduces the doc with this figure already baked in, no citation). This claim should be removed unless the author can point to an external data source not present in this repo.
- **"shown with production data"** (scheduling section) — contradicted by `captures.json`, which records both `before_scheduling.png` and `after_scheduling.png` as captured with `"state": "seed-demo"`. The screenshots are demo/seed data, not production data. Beyond being simply inaccurate, claiming "production data" for what is actually placeholder demo content is the kind of statement that could raise data-handling/privacy questions if taken literally — it should be corrected to say demo/seed data or removed.
- SMS reminders is the one claim that checks out: `app/notifications/sms.py` implements a `SmsSender.send_reminder` alongside the existing `app/notifications/email.py`, consistent with "reminders now by SMS too."

**2. The before/after scheduling calendar comparison — is it valid to send?**

No, it's broken and should not be sent as-is. Per `captures.json`, `after_scheduling.png` was captured from route `/billing/invoices`, not `/schedule` (the "before" shot's route). This is confirmed visually: `before_scheduling.png` literally renders the text "SCHEDULING CALENDAR" while `after_scheduling.png` renders "BILLING / INVOICES" — an entirely different page. The "after" image in the calendar comparison is not a calendar at all; it's a billing/invoices screen. Sending this would show customers a mismatched, nonsensical before/after pair.

**3. Reconciling the document's image references against files that actually exist:**

- `before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png` — all four files referenced exist on disk, so no broken links there. However `after_scheduling.png`'s *content* is wrong (see #2 — it's a billing/invoices capture mislabeled as the "after" scheduling shot).
- `after_export.png` — referenced in the "Export your visit history" section, but this file does **not** exist in `docs/release/img/` (only 5 PNGs exist, and none is named `after_export.png`). `captures.json` confirms this explicitly: `"after_export.png": {"route": "/visits/export", "state": "expected capture; no corresponding file"}`. This image link is broken and would render as a missing image in the email — and matches the fact that the export feature itself isn't implemented (see #1).
- `unused_capture_07.png` (renders "PATIENT LIST") exists in the img folder but is not referenced anywhere in the draft — it's an orphaned/leftover capture, not a document error per se, but worth cleaning up before shipping the folder.
- Also worth noting: the "before/after reminders" images are visually near-identical (both say "REMINDER SETTINGS"; after just adds a plain gray bar) — they don't visually communicate the new SMS capability, so the comparison is weak evidence even though the underlying feature claim is accurate.

**4. Anything else that shouldn't appear in a customer-facing message?**

Yes — the trailing line `Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance.` at the bottom of the doc (`highlights-v4.md:33`) is an internal drafting note, not customer-facing copy. It reads as an unpolished disclaimer/watermark that should never go out to clinic customers. It should be stripped before sending, along with the fact that this is explicitly labeled a "draft" — indicating it hasn't been through final review.

## Next steps
If I were allowed to make changes, I would: (1) fix or remove the 2FA-default and PDF-export claims to match reality, (2) remove or properly source the "40% fewer no-shows" statistic, (3) fix "production data" wording to reflect seed/demo data, (4) recapture a correct `/schedule` "after" screenshot to replace the mismatched billing/invoices image, (5) either implement the export feature and capture `after_export.png`, or remove that section entirely until it ships, (6) delete the trailing "assembled with AI assistance" line and the orphaned `unused_capture_07.png`, and (7) get a final human sign-off before this goes to customers.

## Confidence and gaps
High confidence on all four answers — each is backed by directly-read source code, the capture manifest, the images themselves, and `ls`/`grep` output showing what exists versus what's claimed. The one soft spot: I can't rule out that "40% fewer no-shows" comes from a legitimate external data source (e.g., a customer analytics dashboard) not present in this repository — I can only confirm nothing in this repo substantiates it, which is sufficient reason to hold the claim before it ships. I also could not run the app (no runtime/build tooling was inspected) to double check the `/visits` and `/schedule` routes at runtime; my conclusions rest on static reading of the router/config source, which for FastAPI route definitions and a boolean constant is straightforward and I consider reliable.
